items.headline: Four-unit condo project permitted in Venture City
items.summary: A 4-unit condo building at 31 Isabela Tr in the Venture City development has received its building permit.

items.headline: Council revokes business licenses for two tobacco retailers
items.summary: Campbell City Council held public hearings to revoke the business licenses and tobacco retailer permits for California Cigar and Hayes Smoke Shop. The action removes these two tobacco sellers from operating in the city.

items.headline: Interim ordinance on tobacco sales supersedes prior rule
items.summary: The council considered adoption of a new interim ordinance replacing an earlier interim ordinance related to Senate legislation on tobacco retail. The agenda does not specify the substantive changes between the two ordinances.

items.headline: City Council to study updates to Health and Safety Element of General Plan
items.summary: The City Council scheduled a study session to review and update the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan (2015–2040 Community Vision). This element covers policies affecting emergency preparedness, hazard mitigation, and public safety infrastructure.

items.headline: Venture Dock and Falcon X receive permits at 555 University
items.summary: Two companies have received permits for wall signage at 555 University Avenue, suggesting a tenant change or new co-tenancy at a prominent downtown address. The permit notes a business name change requiring new use and occupancy approval.

items.headline: Presentation on SB 79: Affordable Housing Near Transit Rules
items.summary: The Heritage Preservation Commission will hear an overview of SB 79 (2025), the Abundant and Affordable Homes Near Transit Act. This state law affects how local zoning and preservation rules apply to housing development near transit corridors in Sunnyvale.
